next_steps: Perform immediate 12-lead ECG to characterize the tachycardia
Check serum digoxin level and electrolytes (potassium, magnesium)
Perform urgent point-of-care cardiac ultrasound to evaluate valve function and cardiac output
Initiate telemetry and continuous vital sign monitoring
Obtain stat serum troponin, CBC, and renal function panel
Interrogate ICD to review device event history and rhythm strips
Administer cautious intravenous fluids if clinical suspicion of hypovolemia persists, given hypotension
Prepare for emergent electrical cardioversion if hemodynamically unstable
Immediate 12-lead ECG to differentiate SVT from VT
ICD interrogation to review rhythm logs and device therapy history
Point-of-care ultrasound to assess volume status and biventricular function
Stat laboratory tests: troponin, CBC, electrolytes, renal function, and BNP
Continuous cardiac telemetry and frequent vital sign monitoring
Careful fluid resuscitation with close monitoring for clinical evidence of heart failure
Preparation for electrical cardioversion if clinical instability worsens
